name: Example Steve
subtitle: Demo character - not real data
keyQuestion: What would I do if I knew I couldn't fail?
claudeRead: In a real transitional moment - career search is live now, not theoretical. Side project session showed the right direction produces a different kind of engagement. Main risk: financial pressure shortens the search before the right thing lands. THE INNER CRITIC is active but being noticed faster. Rest is the most significant unmet need.

allies.name: Jordan
allies.desc: Best friend since university. Calls me out gently and shows up consistently.
allies.why: Knows the full version - the messy bits included.

allies.name: Dog
allies.desc: Unconditional support. Not always the most talkative, but always in my corner.

mainQuest.title: Build a Career I Actually Care About
mainQuest.description: Move from work that pays the bills to work that uses real strengths and feels worth getting up for.
mainQuest.whyItMatters: More waking hours at work than anywhere else. If it's hollow, everything else feels thin too.
mainQuest.doneWhen: Earning a sustainable income doing work I'd choose even if I didn't need the money.

nextSteps.text: Identify three roles that match strengths and write down why each one excites me
Have one honest conversation with someone already doing work I admire
Spend two hours this week on a side project that feels genuinely mine

sliderLabels.air: More aware than acting - noticing patterns without changing them yet
sliderLabels.fire: Motivation patchy - flickers of purpose but mostly running on obligation
sliderLabels.water: Some aliveness - running and cooking reliable, rest of week flat
sliderLabels.earth: Reasonably grounded - self-criticism present but not dominating

enemies.name: PROCRASTINATION
enemies.desc: Kicks in whenever a task feels ambiguous or high-stakes. The gap between starting and not starting is enormous.

enemies.name: SELF-DOUBT
enemies.desc: Quiet voice that says others are better qualified. Loudest just before any real attempt.

enemies.name: COMPARISON TRAP
enemies.desc: Measuring a messy middle against others' curated highlight reel. Always loses.

limitingBeliefs.title: THE IMPOSTER
limitingBeliefs.belief: "I'm not smart or talented enough to do the work I actually want to do."
limitingBeliefs.desc: Shows up when applying for jobs or sharing creative work. Reframes learning curves as proof of incompetence.

limitingBeliefs.title: THE PERFECTIONIST
limitingBeliefs.belief: "If I can't do it perfectly, there's no point starting."
limitingBeliefs.desc: Main engine of procrastination. Creates an impossible standard that ensures nothing ships.

limitingBeliefs.title: THE PESSIMIST
limitingBeliefs.belief: "Things rarely work out for me the way they do for other people."
limitingBeliefs.desc: Selective memory that catalogues failures and dismisses wins.

empoweringBeliefs.title: THE PROBLEM SOLVER
empoweringBeliefs.belief: "I can figure out hard things if I stay in the room long enough."
empoweringBeliefs.desc: Track record of working through ambiguous problems is real.

empoweringBeliefs.title: THE LONG GAME
empoweringBeliefs.belief: "Slow progress in the right direction beats fast progress in the wrong one."
empoweringBeliefs.desc: Useful counter to urgency and comparison.

empoweringBeliefs.title: THE BODY KNOWS
empoweringBeliefs.belief: "The aliveness I feel when working on the right thing is data, not coincidence."
empoweringBeliefs.desc: The qualitative difference between the side project and the day job is the clearest signal available.

skills.name: Set Boundaries
skills.whyItMatters: Without this, time and energy get consumed by everyone else's agenda.

skills.name: Ask For Help
skills.description: Reach out before stuck, not after.

skills.name: Stay Consistent
skills.description: Show up even when motivation is absent.
skills.whyItMatters: Running, reading, side project all depend on this.

skills.name: Say No
skills.description: Decline obligations that conflict with priorities.
skills.whyItMatters: Every yes to the wrong thing is a no to something that matters.

skills.name: Problem Solving
skills.description: Break ambiguous problems into workable paths.
skills.whyItMatters: The capability that earns trust fastest.

skills.name: Written Communication
skills.whyItMatters: In async work, writing is the primary signal of competence.

skills.name: Empathy
skills.description: Read what someone needs beneath what they say.

sideQuests.title: Start Running
sideQuests.nextStep: Run three times this week - no distance requirement, just show up
sideQuests.whyItMatters: Physical health is the foundation everything else is built on.

sideQuests.title: Build the Portfolio
sideQuests.nextStep: Publish two case studies by end of March
sideQuests.whyItMatters: The career shift needs proof of work, not just intent.

sideQuests.title: Read 12 Books This Year
sideQuests.nextStep: Finish the current book before starting anything else
sideQuests.whyItMatters: Deep reading slows me down usefully and generates ideas I couldn't alone.

sideQuests.title: Declutter Home
sideQuests.nextStep: Clear the spare room - last section, been sitting for a month

sideQuests.title: Learn Spanish
sideQuests.nextStep: Complete one Duolingo lesson every day for two weeks straight
sideQuests.whyItMatters: Opens travel, connects with family heritage, and is genuinely just for me.

completedQuests.title: Quit Smoking
completedQuests.howResolved: Cold turkey after a chest infection made the cost undeniable. Three months clean.

drains.name: Late-night scrolling
drains.desc: Reliably ends with feeling worse. Eats into sleep.

drains.name: Saying yes to avoid discomfort
drains.desc: Fills the calendar with obligations that don't align with priorities.

drains.name: Skipping the gym when stressed
drains.desc: The moment it would help most is exactly when it gets cut.

flowSources.name: Running
flowSources.desc: First 10 minutes are hard, then the thinking quiets down.

flowSources.name: Cooking
flowSources.desc: Hands busy, mind relaxed. Reliably restorative.

flowSources.name: Music
flowSources.desc: Playing guitar alone - no audience, no performance.

flowSources.name: Time with family
flowSources.desc: Sunday dinners where nothing is at stake.

needs.name: Connection
needs.description: Genuine closeness with people who know the real version. Good with a few, absent with most.

needs.name: Rest
needs.description: Recovery time not immediately colonised by guilt or a to-do list. Significant deficit.

needs.name: Autonomy
needs.description: Control over how time and energy are spent. Better than it was.

needs.name: Recognition
needs.description: Feeling that effort and contribution are seen. Work environment is neutral at best.

values.name: Authenticity
values.description: Living in line with what I actually think and feel, not what looks right from the outside.

values.name: Family
values.description: Being present and reliable for the people who matter most.

values.name: Health
values.description: Physical and mental wellbeing as a non-negotiable foundation.

values.name: Growth
values.description: Getting better at things that matter.

values.name: Financial Security
values.description: Enough stability to make genuine choices - freedom from scarcity anxiety.

entries.title: Two Hours That Felt Like Mine
events: Blocked out Saturday morning for the side project - phone in another room
Two uninterrupted hours on the portfolio site for the first time
Told Jordan about it in the evening
Said no to a colleague asking for help with their presentation
insights: The two hours felt different - less like performance, more like contact with something real
Telling Jordan made it feel more real, not more exposed
Saying no was uncomfortable for about thirty seconds and then fine
PROCRASTINATION didn't fire once - a specific small task made the difference
tensions: Whether the side project is the actual direction or just a way of feeling busy without risking the real thing
One session is not a pattern - Stay Consistent is still unproven here
linkedQuests: Build a Career I Actually Care About

entries.title: The Coffee That Changed the Frame
events: Met Priya for coffee - she made the career switch two years ago
She asked what I was actually good at, not what I wanted to do - different question
Spent the afternoon writing the first honest strengths list
Skipped the gym to finish the list, slept badly
insights: SELF-DOUBT reframed Priya's question as a test I was about to fail - I noticed it this time
The honest strengths list was harder to write than the aspirational one, and more useful
Skipping the gym to finish something is a pattern worth watching
Priya said the transition took longer than expected but she never questioned whether it was right
tensions: Whether I'm choosing roles that actually fit or roles that look like the right answer
Financial pressure is shortening the timeline in ways that might distort the search
linkedQuests: Build a Career I Actually Care About

entries.title: The List That Actually Felt Honest
events: Wrote down three roles that genuinely excite me without immediately dismissing them
Read for 45 minutes before bed, finished the current book
Sat with the Comparison Trap after seeing a former colleague announce a promotion
insights: Writing the list required noticing THE INNER CRITIC in real time and continuing anyway
The promotion announcement triggered COMPARISON TRAP immediately - the pattern is faster than thought
Finishing the book felt like a small win - the Read 12 Books quest is moving without effort
tensions: The three roles feel slightly out of reach - realistic assessment or THE INNER CRITIC curating ambition downward?
Comparison Trap was strong today - worth watching as the search becomes more visible
linkedQuests: Build a Career I Actually Care About

entries.title: Three Months Clean
events: Three months without a cigarette - marked it quietly, told Jordan
Ran twice this week, both times over 20 minutes
Cleared the living room and bedroom sections of the flat
insights: Staying stopped required a quiet decision to stop negotiating with the habit
Quitting and starting to run arrived together - the body responding to being taken seriously
THE INNER CRITIC said the three months don't count because the trigger was external. That's exactly what it does.
tensions: Whether the motivation to run holds once it stops feeling like a reaction to quitting
Rest is still a deficit - evenings feel productive but not restorative

entries.title: The Declutter That Was Also Something Else
events: Cleared the kitchen, hallway, and most of the bedroom over a long weekend
Threw out three bin bags of things kept for reasons that no longer made sense
Set up a proper workspace in the corner of the living room
insights: Clearing the physical space had an immediate effect on mental noise - not metaphorical, genuinely quieter
Most of what I threw away had been kept out of vague obligation, not use
The workspace setup felt like a statement of intent
tensions: The spare room is still untouched - probably means something
The workspace is ready but Deep Work sessions are still short and interrupted
linkedQuests: Build a Career I Actually Care About

boss.name: THE INNER CRITIC
boss.desc: The central voice that frames every setback as evidence of fundamental inadequacy. Feels like a fair assessment. It isn't.
boss.whyBoss: Every other enemy is this one in disguise - Procrastination is the Critic saying you'll fail, Self-Doubt is the Critic questioning your worth, the Comparison Trap is the Critic finding external evidence.

currentEnemies.name: PROCRASTINATION
currentEnemies.desc: Kicks in whenever a task feels ambiguous or high-stakes. Masquerades as preparation.
currentEnemies.trigger: Ambiguous or important tasks with no clear first step
currentEnemies.shortTermBenefit: Protects against the pain of trying and failing. Feels like caution.
currentEnemies.origin: Likely formed around high-stakes performance where effort without success felt worse than not trying.

currentEnemies.name: SELF-DOUBT
currentEnemies.desc: Quiet voice that says others are better qualified. Speaks in first person so it sounds like truth.
currentEnemies.shortTermBenefit: Keeps expectations low so disappointment hurts less. Feels like realism.
currentEnemies.origin: Unknown - may relate to environments where confidence was penalised or not modelled.

currentEnemies.name: COMPARISON TRAP
currentEnemies.desc: Measuring a messy middle against others' curated highlight reel. Always loses.
currentEnemies.trigger: Social media, professional updates, seeing peers succeed
currentEnemies.shortTermBenefit: Creates temporary direction - if others succeeded this way, the path must be real.
currentEnemies.origin: Unknown - the conditions that made external benchmarks feel necessary are not yet clear.

graveyard.name: SUNDAY DREAD
graveyard.howDefeated: Lost its footing when the job ended. Without the thing it was anticipating, it had nothing to attach to.

graveyard.name: THE AVOIDER
graveyard.howDefeated: Named it properly for the first time. Avoidance disguised as being busy. Once it had a name it stopped working as cover.

graveyard.name: INSTAGRAM SPIRAL
graveyard.howDefeated: Deleted the app after noticing the pattern: open it feeling okay, close it feeling worse, every time.

activities.name: Deep Work
activities.description: Focused time on the career transition - research, applications, side project. No multitasking.
activities.whyItMatters: The career I want won't build itself in the margins.

activities.name: Morning Run
activities.whyItMatters: Movement sets the tone for the whole day.

activities.name: Gym Sessions
activities.description: Strength training, three or four times a week.
activities.whyItMatters: Weekly consistency matters more than individual sessions.

achievements.title: Smoke Free
achievements.description: Three months without a cigarette after a decade of trying.

achievements.title: The Declutterer
achievements.description: Cleared 75% of the home in a single sustained push.

achievements.title: Signal Over Noise
achievements.description: Deleted Instagram after noticing the pattern: open it feeling okay, close it feeling worse.

classes.title: Creator
classes.description: Drawn to making things. Finds meaning in the act of creation itself, not just the result.

classes.title: Achiever
classes.description: Goal-oriented and persistent when the goal actually matters. Learning to tell the difference between achievement and validation.

_xpLog.reason: Coffee with Priya - genuine ask for help
Three months smoke-free - Smoke Free achievement
Skipped gym three times - named pattern, not bad luck
